title: Brands Briefing: Why employee-generated content is a priority for brands like Starbucks and Mephisto
tldr: Explores how major brands are systematically prioritizing employee-generated content as an authenticity and reach strategy. Directly relevant to EGC governance and human-centered content.
why_fresh: EGC moving from ad hoc to strategic priority at scale
angle: How to build an EGC program with governance that keeps it human, not corporate-scripted.

title: How Visit Philadelphia turned 3,000 voicemails into 2 million impressions
tldr: A campaign breakdown showing how a simple phone-line mechanic generated massive earned engagement around the Super Bowl. Concrete, human-centered case study.
angle: Why 'analog' participatory formats beat polished content in triggering real audience engagement.

title: 10 brands that won the World Cup on social media without making a big-budget football ad
tldr: Breaks down how brands captured World Cup attention through agile social play rather than expensive TV spots. Multiple mini case studies for content strategists.
angle: The case for agile, low-budget reactive content over tentpole ad spend at big cultural moments.

title: Influencer study: authenticity or unambiguous artificiality, please
tldr: New study finds stylised AI influencers perform as well as real people, while realistic-but-fake ones fall into the uncanny valley. Reveals shifting audience psychology around synthetic media.
angle: Why audiences forgive obvious AI but reject fake realism — the authenticity paradox for brands.

title: How America's newspapers lost their voice
tldr: A historical narrative on the decline of editorial courage in local newspapers, from a fearless 1963 editor to today's muted press. Rich fodder on corporate narrative and voice.
why_fresh: fresh historical lens on media voice erosion
angle: What brands and publishers lose when they optimize away a distinct editorial voice.

title: With its third issue, audio magazine Signal Hill is transmitting further than ever
tldr: A podcast structured like a magazine — issues, dispatches, features, profiles — is redefining audio content architecture. A genuinely novel format experiment.
angle: Borrowing editorial architecture from print to rethink podcast and audio content structure.

title: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
tldr: Substack integrates Pangram to flag AI-generated text across posts and comments. A signal about the platform value of human-authored content.
why_fresh: new platform feature signaling human-content premium
angle: As AI detection goes mainstream, human authorship becomes a badge — how creators should lean into it.

title: AI search does not care about your website
tldr: Argues that AI-driven answer engines are decoupling discovery from traditional websites, forcing a rethink of content and SEO strategy. Timely provocation on AEO.
angle: Rebuilding content strategy for a world where the website is no longer the destination.

title: Albertsons' new 'micro sitcom' is a big deal for retail media
tldr: Albertsons and P&G launch a scripted episodic branded-entertainment series, signaling retail media's move into narrative content. Concrete new-format campaign.
why_fresh: new branded-entertainment format in retail media
angle: When retailers become studios: the rise of scripted branded content and what it demands of storytellers.

title: The World Cup of misinformation
tldr: Chronicles how a false on-air death claim about Messi's father spread during the World Cup, illustrating live-media misinformation dynamics. Sharp media-consumption lens.
why_fresh: live misinformation case study during major event
angle: How real-time media consumption amplifies unverified claims — and what comms teams must pre-plan.

title: What employees need when everything around them is changing
tldr: Practical framework on building employee trust through AI shifts, onboarding, and disruption. Directly useful for internal comms and change management.
angle: Internal comms as the trust layer during AI-driven workplace change.

title: Clipping Comes to the Movies: How Micro-Influencer Agency SGG Media Helps the Ground Game for Hollywood Marketing
tldr: Details how 'clipping' — repurposing content into micro-clips via micro-influencers — is now a Hollywood marketing tactic. A fresh look at micro-content distribution mechanics.
angle: Clipping economies: how micro-content armies replace the trailer as the unit of marketing.

title: Why media relations just came full circle – and how to get journalists to actually notice you
tldr: Argues PR fundamentals are back in vogue, now complicated by AI in the newsroom loop. Practical, timely take on earned media.
why_fresh: useful but partly evergreen PR advice
angle: How AI in newsrooms is reshaping — not killing — human media relationships.

title: Only 45% of marketers gain quality from AI, WARC and TikTok find
tldr: New WARC/TikTok data shows less than half of marketers report quality gains from AI, tempering the hype. Useful performance benchmark.
why_fresh: new data challenging AI quality assumptions
angle: The AI quality gap: why speed gains don't equal content quality gains.
